goal: Step 4: agent guidance task taxonomy — context-first vs code-first task list, template v10

summary.title: Agent guidance template v10: product positioning line + lint surface mention
summary.what: Bumps the embedded agent guidance template from v9 to v10. Adds the spec §1 product positioning line ('Mainline is a git-native intent memory layer for AI-assisted engineering. It gives coding agents the historical why before they inspect the current what.') as the lead paragraph. Adds a Step 5 'lint' subsection inside the seal-the-task workflow that points agents at `mainline lint <intent_id>` (advisory, what it catches, why it matters for retrieval quality). Pre-existing pieces (3-column task taxonomy, hooks-as-context-provider rule) retained.
summary.why: Spec §9 Step 1 (Product thesis 收口) and Step 4 (Agent Guidance 强化) had two concrete deliverables not yet reflected in the template: the canonical positioning line, and a pointer at `mainline lint` (which shipped in PR #65 but had no agent-facing doc). The template is the only doc every agent reads on first contact, so positioning + tool discovery have to live here.
summary.user_goal: When an agent reads AGENTS.md for the first time, it should immediately understand what Mainline is for AND know `mainline lint` exists as a quality check on its seal output.

decisions.chose: Two surgical additions only (positioning line + lint subsection); leave the existing taxonomy and hooks sections untouched.
decisions.rationale: Pre-existing template already had the 3-column task taxonomy (richer than spec §9 Step 4's 2-column ask) and the hook policy (more precise than spec's 'soft remind not hard block'). Re-writing what was already correct would just churn the prose without adding value.

decisions.point: where to place the lint mention
decisions.chose: Inside 'When the task is complete' as Step 5, after seal --submit.
decisions.rationale: Lint runs against the sealed payload, so it makes sense as the step right after submit. Placing it at the top of the file (as a feature highlight) would imply lint is more central than it actually is — it's an advisory tool, not part of the agent loop.

decisions.chose: Explicitly call lint 'advisory' and explain its load-bearing role (seal quality controls retrieval quality).
decisions.rationale: Without the why, agents would treat lint as optional polish. With the why ('low-quality seals pollute future mainline context results'), the optional cost has a clear payoff.

rejected.alternative: Re-write the task-priority section to match spec §9 Step 4's exact 2-column format.
rejected.reason: Existing 3-column version is strictly better — it adds a 'Mainline-first preferred' middle tier for things that aren't always context-first but aren't trivially direct-code either (bug fixes, new features, API behaviour changes). Reverting would lose that nuance.

rejected.alternative: Add lint as a top-level section.
rejected.reason: Lint is one tool in a workflow, not a workflow stage. Top-level placement implies parity with start/append/seal/check; it doesn't have that weight.

risks: Two consecutive template bumps (v9 then v10 in two PRs) means agents who synced after v8 now see two update_available cycles instead of one. Acceptable: the migrate flow is idempotent and agents only see this on first `agents check` after the bump.

anti_patterns.what: Bumping the template version without changing body content the agent will care about
anti_patterns.why: Triggers a no-op migration cycle for every existing repo and trains agents to ignore the update_available signal. Each version bump must carry a real reader-visible change.

followups: Step 5: agent eval harness with the 8 fixtures from spec §9 — the validation experiment that decides whether the product thesis holds.
Steps 6-10 (governance, review context, long-term memory, collaboration coherence, hub polish) — all build on the agent-guidance + lint + retrieval foundation now in place.
Consider auto-running lint inside `mainline status` for the active draft once a draft has a structured summary (currently drafts don't, so lint emits an info notice).

architectural_claims: The agent guidance template is the contract for what agents are told about Mainline; new product surfaces (lint, hub) require a template version bump that mentions them or they remain effectively undiscoverable to agents.
Product positioning (spec §1) belongs in the agent-facing template too, not just in product-thesis docs — it shapes how the agent frames what it is reading.
behavioral_changes: Agent guidance template at v10; existing repos see update_available on `mainline agents check`.
On migration, AGENTS.md gains the positioning lead paragraph and a lint step in the seal workflow.

data_model_changes.name: agents-md template version
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Bumped to v10. mainline agents update migrates in place; user content outside the markers preserved.

migration_notes: Run `mainline agents update` to pick up the v10 template.
